meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drifting in tutorial hell. Use this advanced checklist to implement data-driven analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your SaaS.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start measuring. By integrating event-based analytics directly into your MVP's core loops, you transition from guessing to data-backed iteration. Use this workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code to instrument your product, track user retention, and optimize for real revenue generation.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the promise of another 'ultimate' tutorial, yet your MRR remains at zero. You are trapped in the infinite loop of consumption, building features no one asked for because you lack the feedback loop to prove their value. This is the graveyard of startups: the 'build-and-pray' methodology.

It is time to kill the guesswork. True founders don't build in the dark; they build, measure, and pivot based on cold, hard event data. This checklist isn't about code tutorials; it's about building a telemetry engine that tells you exactly why your users are churning or why they are upgrading. Let’s turn your MVP into a revenue-generating machine.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Identify the one action that correlates most with user success. Without this, your analytics are just noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the top 3 high-intent user actions that signal product value. Create a schema for tracking these events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify core value events and generate a tracking schema.ts file'

steps.title: Implement Lightweight Telemetry Middleware
steps.description: Don't bloat your app. Use middleware to intercept events without polluting your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular analytics middleware for my Next.js stack that captures user ID, event name, and metadata without blocking the main thread.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create middleware.ts for event tracking and integrate it into the API route handler'

steps.title: Instrument Key Conversion Funnels
steps.description: Track the path from landing page to payment. Every drop-off point is a potential revenue leak.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the user journey from signup to payment and generate tracking calls for each step of the funnel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add analytics.track calls to signup and checkout components'

steps.title: Automate Error and Latency Tracking
steps.description: If your app is slow or broken, users leave. Track performance as part of your analytics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a performance monitor script that logs latency for my API endpoints and fires an event if response time exceeds 500ms.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement performance monitoring in the base controller'

steps.title: Build Automated Revenue Dashboards
steps.description: Visualize your data. If you can't see the revenue trend, you aren't managing the business.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard configuration file that aggregates my tracked events into a view showing DAU, conversion rate, and churn.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate dashboard.config.json and setup the aggregation query'

pro_tips: Never track everything; track only the events that directly influence your primary business goal.
Use semantic naming conventions for events (e.g., 'User_Clicked_Upgrade_Plan') to make SQL queries readable.
Always include a 'context' object in your event payload to capture device, browser, and location data automatically.
Sanitize PII (Personally Identifiable Information) at the source before sending data to third-party analytics providers.
Set up automated alerts for significant drops in conversion rates so you catch revenue leaks before they become critical.
content.next_steps: Once your tracking is live, spend 48 hours observing real user behavior. Use the data to identify your top 10% of users and interview them to understand why they stay, then double down on those features.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for growth. The data is waiting to tell you what your users actually want. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
